label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to analyze this ethical dilemma about digital image manipulation in advertising and whether advertisers should continue doing it. Let me start by breaking down the problem. First, the issue is that advertisers use digital tools to change how people look in their ads. This can make models look thinner, more muscular, have clearer skin, etc. The problem is that this makes the images unrealistic, and when people see these, especially young people, they might feel bad about their own bodies. That can lead to low self-esteem and even eating disorders. The question is, should advertisers keep using these manipulations even though they might cause harm? Alright, so I need to look at this from different angles. Maybe start with the utilitarian perspective. That's about the greatest good for the most people. If advertisers stop manipulating images, maybe people won't
